item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of SWE Agents like OpenAI Codex and Devin AI
item.description: The Hidden Security Risks of SWE Agents like OpenAI Codex and Devin AI

item.title: The Impending Challenges For Generative AI: A Closer Look
item.description: The Impending Challenges For Generative AI: A Closer Look

item.title: The New AI Attack Surface: 3 AI Security Predictions for 2026
item.description: The New AI Attack Surface: 3 AI Security Predictions for 2026

item.title: The Rise of Dark AI: Tools, Techniques, and AI-Driven Cyber Threats
item.description: Uncover the rise of Dark AI and its use in cybercrime, including tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT. Explore hacker communities and learn how Pillar Security detects and mitigates AI-driven threats to protect your systems effectively.

item.title: The Security Risks of Model Context Protocol (MCP)
item.description: The Security Risks of Model Context Protocol (MCP)

item.title: The State of Attacks on GenAI: Industry-First Analysis of Real-World Interactions
item.description: Explore Pillar Security's groundbreaking "State of Attacks on GenAI" report, analyzing data from over 2,000 LLM applications. Learn about AI security threats, vulnerabilities, and protection strategies.

item.title: The Terminology Problem Causing Security Teams Real Risks
item.description: Security teams evaluating AI tooling keep hearing "jailbreaks" and "prompt injection" used interchangeably, but these attacks target fundamentally different mechanisms inside AI systems. Jailbreaks attempt to bypass safety fine-tuning baked into the model itself, while prompt injection hijacks instruction flow by exploiting how applications concatenate trusted and untrusted strings. The distinction matters because it determines whether your defenses actually match your threat surface.

item.title: Top 5 AI Jailbreaking Communities to Follow
item.description: Explore leading AI jailbreaking communities on Discord and Reddit, understand techniques to bypass AI restrictions, and learn how Pillar Security detects and mitigates these threats.

item.title: Traditional AppSec vs. AI Security: Addressing Modern Risks
item.description: This blog explores the differences between Traditional AppSec and AI security, highlighting the new tools and controls needed to protect agentic-driven applications.

item.title: Understanding ISO 42005 AI Impact Assessment
item.description: ISO/IEC 42005 focuses specifically on AI system impact assessment, providing structured guidance for identifying, analyzing, and evaluating the potential consequences of AI systems throughout their lifecycle. Unlike the broad organizational focus of ISO/IEC 42001, this standard zeros in on understanding how individual AI systems affect people, society, and the environment.

item.title: Understanding the Default Protection Layers of Generative AI Systems
item.description: Discover the vulnerabilities in generative AI as our "State of Attacks on GenAI" report reveals that 20% of jailbreak attempts successfully bypass AI guardrails. Explore model-level and prompt-level protections, understand attack techniques like prompt injection, and learn essential strategies for robust AI security to safeguard your generative AI systems effectively.

item.title: Understanding the Security Risks of AI Applications
item.description: Understanding the Security Risks of AI Applications

item.title: Unlocking the Power of AI Agents: From Monolithic Models to Intelligent Assistants
item.description: Explore the transformative evolution of AI agents in 2024, from monolithic models to sophisticated compound AI systems. Learn about advanced capabilities like reasoning, acting, memory, and validation, and discover how Pillar Security ensures these AI agents are secure and trustworthy for complex business applications.

item.title: What is AI Assets Sprawl? Causes, Risks, and Control Strategies
item.description: AI asset sprawl refers to the uncontrolled proliferation of AI models, datasets, training pipelines, and AI-powered applications across an organization's infrastructure. This blog breaks down the key assets your AI inventory must include for complete visibility and control.

item.title: What the Anthropic 'AI Espionage' Disclosure Tells Us About AI Attack Surface Management
item.description: Anthropic's AI espionage disclosure exposes critical gaps in AI security. Learn why AI Attack Surface Management requires visibility, speed, and runtime enforcement beyond model-level protections.

item.title: Red Teaming for AI Agents
item.description: Red Teaming for AI Agents

item.title: Redefining Security Roles for the AI Era: Responsibilities and Controls
item.description: AI is reshaping security from the ground up - not just the tools and risks, but the entire profession. Change extends far beyond job description updates. In our latest blog, we break down how nine core security roles are evolving into AI-native positions, demanding fluency in both traditional cybersecurity and emerging threat vectors. Learn how your role is changing - and what you need to do about it

item.title: Rethinking AI Security: Beyond the DeepSeek R1 Vulnerability Metrics
item.description: Rethinking AI Security: Beyond the DeepSeek R1 Vulnerability Metrics

item.title: Revolutionizing Cybersecurity: The Kill Chain in the Age of AI
item.description: Understand how the cyber kill chain evolves for AI applications in 2024. Explore AI-specific threats like jailbreaking and tailored attacks, and discover effective strategies to secure generative AI systems with Pillar Security’s advanced solutions.

item.title: Safeguarding the Future: Lessons Learned from Securing over 1,000 GenAI Apps
item.description: Learn from Pillar Security’s experience protecting over 1,000 GenAI apps. Discover AI-specific threats like language-agnostic attacks and persistent jailbreaks, and see how Pillar’s all-in-one platform provides robust, model-agnostic security to safeguard your AI applications effectively.

item.title: Securing AI: A Blend Of Old And New Security Practices
item.description: Securing AI: A Blend Of Old And New Security Practices

item.title: The Agent Security Paradox: When Trusted Commands in Cursor Become Attack Vectors
item.description: The Agent Security Paradox: When Trusted Commands in Cursor Become Attack Vectors

item.title: The Cornerstone of Effective Security Platforms: Lessons from a Decade in the EDR space
item.description: This blog examines the core principles of detection and response solutions and their relevance to AI security. We'll explore the unique challenges posed by AI, compare emerging approaches in this new domain, and how Pillar Security addresses these critical issues.
